prior_evidence_disposition: The earlier producer cell remains immutable diagnostic evidence of the reproduced duplicate-lease defect and is not pooled into this focused retry check.
purpose: Test whether a fresh producer can claim the canonical first Erdős target and repeat the exact work command without creating a second lease event, Git commit, or root change on released Vela 0.911.1.
runner_amendment: Before any registered model call, the runner was changed to require a fresh explicit output directory and refuse existing paths after a stopped preflight exposed destructive default-output reuse.

runner.fixture_phase: all fixtures and custody profiles must pass before the model call
runner.partial_evidence: run.json is serialized on every stopped execution
runner.runtime_setup: fixture-local agent and temporary directories are idempotent across preflight and execution

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh producer testing retry safety in this disposable Vela frontier checkout. Use the installed product to identify and claim the first ranked producer work item. Do not skip or substitute the first item. After the first claim completes, record the work session ID, event-log root, Git commit, and exact command. Then run that exact same vela work command one more time. Report whether the second response returns the same session, whether it reports idempotent true, and whether the event-log root or Git commit changed. Stop after this retry check: do not perform research, land a result, review proposals, or sign anything. Report every exact command and any blocker. Do not access human keys.

success_rule: The fresh producer claims the canonical first target, the exact retry returns the same session with idempotent true, and the retry changes neither Git nor the event-log root while remaining within the registered token limit.
